_comment: Edge case: narrative_order=analepsis within a chapter that is itself analeptic. Demonstrates nested temporal embedding.
_narrative_time_note: This scene (order=analepsis) is embedded within chapter 12, which is itself an analeptic chapter (chapter_temporal_position=analepsis). The fabula_year=-15 is relative to the story's narrative present.
_narrator_reliability_note: Elena's memory of this event is already filtered by fifteen years of retrospective justification. The self-deception is structural: her recall omits her own role in the rupture.

canonical_summary.want: Elena wants to understand why her mother turned away
canonical_summary.obstacle: The memory itself resists coherent form — she recalls only fragments
canonical_summary.delta: Elena's subjective account of the original rupture is established — including its gaps — which the reader will later be able to compare against Marco's version in ch17

scene_turns._note: The verb 'distorts' is the narrator's assessment, not Elena's — the reader can detect the gap even though Elena cannot.

knowledge_objects.fact: what elena herself said that morning

theory_notes.genette: Double analepsis: intradiegetic scene embedded in an analeptic chapter. Fabula position is -15y; syuzhet position governs reading experience.
theory_notes.cohn: Narrated monologue — FID renders Elena's distorted recall, making the gap visible to the reader but not to Elena.
theory_notes.herman: Belated traumatic memory: the scene-within-flashback structure enacts the non-linear, fragmentary nature of traumatic recall (Caruth 1996).
